description_long: This rifle features a robust wooden stock with a rich brown grain, seamlessly attached to a metallic receiver and barrel assembly. The receiver and barrel are constructed from dark, weathered steel, showing signs of wear and utilitarian use. A prominent drum magazine is mounted beneath the receiver, indicating a high-capacity feeding system. The barrel is perforated with cooling holes along its length, and the front sight is a simple post, while the rear sight is a notch integrated into the receiver. The trigger guard and trigger are metallic, and the overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century military firearms. The combination of wood and metal gives the rifle a classic, rugged appearance suitable for urban or historical settings.
description: A classic rifle with a wooden stock, dark steel receiver, and a large drum magazine, featuring a perforated barrel and simple iron sights.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full wooden stock, metallic receiver, drum magazine, and perforated barrel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular drum magazine, barrel opening, and front sight post.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden stock, receiver, and drum magazine from the other profile.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the receiver and barrel.
